emergency.title: Emergency & Help In KOREA Guide
emergency.subtitle: Official Protection System Guide for Foreign Tourists
emergency.swipeTip: Swipe cards to see detailed instructions
emergency.lostTip: Recommended Action: Check your recent credit card history.
emergency.hospitalTip: Dial 119 directly; GPS will track your location automatically.

h1.title: Free 119 Ambulance
h1.desc: In Korea, 119 ambulance and medical transport are completely FREE for everyone, including foreign tourists. Do not hesitate to call.

h2.title: 24/7 Translation
h2.desc: 119 provides 3-way interpretation in 16 languages. Just call and speak your language; they will connect a translator immediately.

h3.title: Visit Local Clinics (Walk-in)
h3.desc: For mild illness like cold or stomachache, visit local 'Clinics (의원)'. Walk-in is welcome, very fast, and extremely affordable professional care.

l1.title: Go Back to the Place
l1.desc: Korea has a very safe culture. Your item is likely still there or kept by the staff. Go back to the cafe or restaurant first.

l2.title: Lost on Subway?
l2.desc: Note: 1. Arrival Time 2. Direction 3. Train Car Number (number on the floor). Tell this to the station customer center immediately.

l3.title: Police LOST112 System
l3.desc: Register your lost item or search at www.lost112.go.kr. Recovered items are kept safely for 6 months by law.

p1.title: Get a Police Report
p1.desc: Priority 1: Visit the nearest police station for a 'Police Report'. Without this, you cannot get an emergency passport or leave Korea.

p2.title: Photos & Embassy
p2.desc: Bring 2 passport photos and the Police Report to your Embassy. They will issue an emergency travel document.

p3.title: For Insurance Claims
p3.desc: Keep your Police Report safely. It is the core evidence needed for traveler's insurance claims for stolen luggage back home.

labels.verifiedFor: Verified for Foreign Cards · Instant e-Ticket

preparing.title: Transport Guide Updating
preparing.desc: We are verifying regional transport data to provide more accurate information.
preparing.linkNotice: Please refer to the official sources for general transport info:

general.qrDesc: If you have an Express Train voucher, enter the serial number at the Automatic Ticket Machine to receive a physical QR ticket.
general.intlTaxiDesc: Fixed fares by zone (70,000~95,000 KRW). Drivers speak English, Japanese, Chinese.
general.surchargeDesc: When taking a taxi from ICN to Seoul, always pick a taxi with a 'SEOUL' license plate to avoid the 30% out-of-city surcharge.
general.lateNightDesc: Late-night buses N6000, N6001, N6002 are available from Terminal 1, 1F Platform 6A.
general.expressRouteDesc: Stops at major stations only. Best for Gangnam.
general.applePayDesc: Top-up via 'Foreigner' menu in Mobile T-money.
general.applePayNotice: Apple Pay: Charging T-money via foreign-issued cards may be restricted. WOWPASS physical card is recommended.
general.luggageHandsFree: Send your bags from the airport to your hotel and travel hands-free.

mobility.title: Mobility Apps for Foreigners
mobility.desc: No Korean phone authentication required. Use international cards directly.
mobility.krideBenefits: No Korean phone needed · Auto-pay ready

luggage.title: Hands-Free Luggage Delivery
luggage.desc: Send your bags from ICN to your hotel and travel light.
luggage.notSupported: Direct delivery is not supported for this hotel. Use external SAFEX hubs.
